title: // verilator isolate_assignments causes error
body:
---


Author Name: **Jeremy Bennett** (@jeremybennett)
Original Redmine Issue: 60 from https://www.veripool.org
Original Date: 2009-01-26


---

I tried adding the comment @/* verilator isolate_assignments */@ to a statement, to cure an UNOPTFLAT warning:

```
wire rx_kbd_data_ready;  /* verilator isolate_assignments */ 

```

This was rejected with the error:

```
%Error: rtl/verilog/ps2/ps2_top.v:145: syntax error, unexpected /*verilator isolate_assignments*/, expecting IDENTIFIER
%Error: Cannot continue
%Error: Command Failed /home/jeremy/tools/verilator/verilator-3.700/verilator_bin -trace -f v_processed.scr -language 1364-2001 -sc
make: *** [obj_dir/Vorpsoc.mk] Error 10

```

I've tried shuffling the comment around in all sorts of ways. I thought it might be due to placing it by a wire (rather than a reg), but moving to a register in the loop does not cure the problem, although the error is now different. Using:

```
reg rx_data_ready;  /* verilator isolate_assignments */

``` 

Gives the error:

```
Error: rtl/verilog/ps2/ps2_keyboard.v:200: syntax error, unexpected /*verilator isolate_assignments*/
%Error: Cannot continue
%Error: Command Failed /home/jeremy/tools/verilator/verilator-3.700/verilator_bin -trace -f v_processed.scr -language 1364-2001 -sc
make: *** [obj_dir/Vorpsoc.mk] Error 10

```


I think I'm following the advice in the user guide,
